genetic_lineage.overview: Touriga Nacional is indigenous to the Dão region of Portugal. DNA analysis has not identified its parentage, confirming it as an ancient Portuguese variety.

parents.name: Unknown
parents.notes: No parentage established. Indigenous to the Dão region.

offspring.name: Touriga Franca
offspring.notes: Cross with Mourisco Tinto. Important Douro variety.

genetic_lineage.genetic_significance: Touriga Nacional's role as parent of Touriga Franca connects it to the other key variety of Port production. The variety's genetic integrity has been preserved through centuries of Portuguese winemaking tradition.

clones.name: Clone TN-101
clones.characteristics: Standard certified clone with good color, structure, and aromatic intensity.

clones.name: Clone TN-112
clones.characteristics: Selected for enhanced floral aromatics and violet notes.

clones.name: Dão Heritage Selection
clones.characteristics: Traditional massal selections from old Dão vineyards. Maximum elegance and complexity.

clones.name: Douro Selection
clones.characteristics: Selections adapted to hot Douro conditions. Concentrated and powerful.

terroir.effect: The defining soil of the Dão. Poor, acidic granite produces elegant wines with fine tannins and aromatic complexity.
The dominant soil of the Douro. Heat-retentive schist produces powerful, concentrated wines.
Found in parts of Alentejo. Produces rounder, more approachable wines.

susceptibilities.notes: Can suffer poor fruit set in some conditions.

resistances.notes: Well-adapted to dry conditions, essential in the Douro.

disease_profile.general_notes: Touriga Nacional is a low-yielding variety with small, thick-skinned berries. It produces deeply colored, tannic wines with intense violet aromatics. Originally confined to the Dão, it has spread throughout Portugal and is now grown internationally. Widely considered Portugal's greatest red variety.

regional_expressions.style_summary: The historic home of Touriga Nacional, producing elegant, aromatic wines with fine tannins. The granite soils and moderate climate create wines of finesse rather than power. Often compared to Burgundy for its emphasis on elegance.

Portugal's most prestigious region for dry reds, also home to Port. Touriga Nacional produces powerful, concentrated wines. Both varietal and blended styles are common. The hot climate creates bigger, bolder wines than the Dão.

Portugal's largest wine region is increasingly planting Touriga Nacional. The hot climate produces ripe, approachable wines that are softer than Douro examples. Good value alternatives to more prestigious regions.

Touriga Nacional is considered the finest variety for Vintage and Vintage Port. It contributes intense color, structure, and violet aromatics to blends. The variety's concentration makes it ideal for fortified wines.
